questions.question: Which part of the brain acts as the 'fear center' and triggers the initial alarm during a panic attack?
questions.explanation: The amygdala is the brain's fear center. During a panic attack, it detects a perceived 'threat' and sounds the alarm in milliseconds, before your conscious thinking brain can evaluate whether the threat is real.

questions.question: What is the typical timeline for a panic attack to reach peak intensity?
questions.explanation: Panic attack symptoms typically reach their peak intensity within 5-10 minutes. Most symptoms resolve within 30 minutes as the adrenaline is metabolized and the parasympathetic nervous system engages.

questions.question: What causes the tingling and numbness sensations during a panic attack?

options.text: Hyperventilation causing temporary changes in blood CO2 levels

questions.explanation: Tingling and numbness during a panic attack are caused by hyperventilation, which temporarily changes blood CO2 levels. This slight shift in blood chemistry is harmless and reverses within minutes.

questions.question: Which of the following is required for a diagnosis of panic disorder, beyond having panic attacks?

options.text: Having at least one panic attack per day
Persistent worry about future attacks and behavior changes to avoid them
Experiencing panic attacks only in specific locations
Having panic attacks triggered exclusively by external threats

questions.explanation: Panic disorder develops when you have recurrent unexpected panic attacks, persistent worry about having more attacks (at least 1 month), and you change your behavior to avoid situations where panic might occur. A single panic attack alone does not constitute panic disorder.

questions.question: Why is understanding the physiology of panic attacks considered therapeutic?

options.text: It eliminates the possibility of ever having another panic attack
It allows you to physically control your amygdala
It reduces the fear of the panic itself, which is what keeps the cycle going
It replaces the need for any other treatment or coping strategies

questions.explanation: Research shows that psychoeducation — understanding what panic attacks are — is a powerful first step. When you know your symptoms have physiological explanations and are not dangerous, you reduce the fear of panic itself, which is what maintains the panic cycle.

title: What Happens During a Panic Attack - Quiz
